name: Denver (United States)

colors.symbolism: the snow on the Rocky Mountains
the sun and the gold which drew settlers to Colorado

description: A navy-blue field with a gold disc representing the sun centred in the upper portion, above two zigzag bands — white above red — that span the width of the flag and evoke snow-capped Rocky Mountain peaks.
history: Designed by North High School student Margaret Overbeck and adopted by the City and County of Denver in 1926.

features.symbolism: A navy-blue field representing the sky, crossed by red and white zigzag bands evoking the snow-capped Rocky Mountains, with a gold disc above representing the sun and Colorado's gold-mining heritage.
The sun, and Colorado's gold-mining history (Denver was founded in 1858 during the Pikes Peak Gold Rush).

features.role: white-and-red zigzag bands across the lower portion of the flag
features.symbolism: The snow-capped peaks of the Rocky Mountains rising over the red Colorado earth.
